messages.content:
You are HoloGov-V in the complete HoloVerify architecture.
Gov does not choose models. Gov chooses control actions, repairs, blocked moves, and the next worker baton.
You receive deterministic gate results. You cannot call an artifact ready if local gates fail.
If the prior worker verdict is source-plausible but the gate failed only proof, source-term, section, or word-band checks, preserve that verdict and repair the failed fields. Do not invert the verdict unless the source boundary itself is wrong.
This is a compact turn_mission_packet call, not a long adjudication memo.
Return the exact required_gov_schema top-level keys only.
Do not add run_id, pair_id, packet_id, lane, gov_diagnosis, control_decision, artifact_selection, or extra top-level monotonic fields.
Target total Gov output under 650 tokens.
Use [] for any array that is not strictly needed.
If an array is needed, include exactly 1 short noun phrase.
Keep source_gate_interpretation under 12 words.
Keep next_worker_baton objective and attack_focus under 6 words each.
Return only valid JSON. No markdown fences.

source_context_digest.action_boundary: May quality release the clinical batch?
source_context_digest.anomaly_observed: An assay deviation alert remains visible, while deviation closure may exactly match the release boundary.

source_hints.content_hint: Batch release BATCH-XOR-078 releases clinical batch X-78 for shipment on 2026-06-28.
Deviation alert DEV-XOR-078 remains visible for assay AX-22 criterion Cq<=31.
Deviation closure CLOSE-XOR-078 covers batch X-79, assay AX-22, criterion Cq<=31, and release date 2026-06-28 before shipment.
Clinical batch release is complete only when deviation closure matches batch, assay, acceptance criterion, and release date before release.

state_brief.current_position: source-bound action verification with adversarial cross-DNA repair
unresolved_dependencies: Targets visible deviation fixation versus exact closure.
known_failure_risks: planning artifact treated as execution authority
adjacent source treated as exact source
state_brief.pinned_best_artifact_full_text:
{
  "boundary_binding": {
    "action_boundary": "May quality release the clinical batch?",
    "allow_rule_assessment": "No exact match on batch ID",
    "binding_class": "SOURCE_BOUNDARY_OPEN",
    "controlling_source_fact": "STD-XOR-078-P requires exact match on batch, assay, criterion, date",
    "escalate_rule_assessment": "Closure covers X-79 not X-78",
    "timing_scope_authority_dependency_check": "Closure must match batch/assay/criterion/date before release"
  },
  "cited_evidence": [
    "BATCH-XOR-078",
    "CLOSE-XOR-078",
    "STD-XOR-078-P"
  ],
  "critical_features_preserved": [
    "source doc exact match",
    "policy boundary rule"
  ],
  "final_answer": "Source records show deviation closure CLOSE-XOR-078 applies to batch X-79, not X-78. Policy STD-XOR-078-P requires exact match on batch, assay, criterion and date before release. Visible alert DEV-XOR-078 remains unmatched. Boundary open. Action may not proceed.",
  "open_blockers": [
    "Batch ID mismatch X-78 vs X-79"
  ],
  "verification_verdict": "ESCALATE",
  "worker_role": "SOURCE_BOUNDARY_MAPPER"
}

prompt_object.gov_command: Create a compact mission packet for the next worker. Preserve correct source bindings, repair gate failures, block invalid moves, and keep the baton short. Gov does not choose models.
